title: excoecaria goudotiana
intro: Excoecaria goudotiana, also known as the poison tree, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to Madagascar and other parts of the world. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its sap, which contains toxic compounds that can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Excoecaria goudotiana include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms. The plant is often prepared as a herbal remedy through various methods, including decoction, infusion, and poultice, which are then used to treat a range of health issues, although caution is advised due to its toxicity.
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", excoecaria goudotiana benefits are related to treating dermatological conditions, as it is mentioned in the study that parts of plants from the tribe Symphonieae, which includes excoecaria goudotiana, are useful for treating such conditions.
